stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of vehicle speed using a speedometer. The measurements have a mean of 157.3 km/h and a standard deviation of 21.0 km/h. However, calibration tests reveal that a speedometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 1.1% and 7.0%. Estimate the true vehicle speed and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 157.3 km/h. After correcting for the 4.0% bias: ≈ 163.9 km/h. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.1%-7.0%) and sampling variability: [145.7, 182.4]. Point estimate: 163.9 km/h.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
